title: HolyCode overhaul: sleev, opencode 1.17.11, background-agents, removed auth features
body:
Complete overhaul of HolyCode Docker image:

Added:
- opencode-ai bumped 1.15.13 → 1.17.11
- sleev CLI + wrapper (renames real sleev to sleev.real, wrapper triggers s6-svc -u after gateway binary appears)
- S6 sleev service (run: direct gateway binary exec, finish: exit 125 when binary missing)
- unique-names-generator (background-agents dependency)
- background-agents.ts plugin (from ocx repo, ships to ~/.config/opencode/plugin/ via bootstrap)

Removed:
- Hermes/Paperclip references from compose files
- Claude Auth (curl claude.ai/install.sh)
- oh-my-openagent (skill directory + entrypoint toggles)
- CLIProxyAPI (provider config block + cliproxyapi service)

Bootstrap now copies shipped plugins to user home on first boot.

title: Added AFT (cortexkit/aft) to HolyCode image
body:
Added AFT (sensorimotor cortex) to the HolyCode Docker image:

- npm packages: @cortexkit/aft (CLI) + @cortexkit/aft-opencode (plugin)
- libonnxruntime (apt) for semantic code search (aft_search) — CPU-only, negligible RAM
- Plugin registered in shipped opencode.json
- Rust binary auto-downloads on first agent use (~30MB cache)

AFT replaces built-in read/write/edit/apply_patch with tree-sitter-backed, symbol-aware versions. Additional tools: outline, zoom, search (semantic+lexical), call graph, codebase health, safety/undo, bash compression, background tasks, PTY.
